body.title: worker_foreman save_tasks race: stale bulk save overwrites concurrent status changes
body.result: Race-safe reset: targeted reset_stale_in_progress() with WHERE status='in_progress' guard; prevents zombie loop when concurrent setstatus/close races the daemon-dead reset. 2 tests added.
body.description:
**Problem:** worker_foreman.py's daemon-dead branch calls `_cc_queue.save_tasks(tasks)` with a stale full-task load. Race: (1) worker_foreman loads all tasks (ticket=in_progress), (2) setstatus/close cancels the ticket in DB, (3) worker_foreman saves its stale list → overwrites the terminal status with "sprint". Then cmd_next claims it → in_progress. reset_stale resets to sprint again. Loop repeats indefinitely.

**Evidence from T-claude-1 zombie history:** setstatus→cancelled at 23:17, reset_stale finds in_progress at 23:24 (7-min gap). No log entry between those events — worker_foreman save_tasks does not call _log for each ticket it resets. T-claude-1 was cancelled/closed 5+ times since May 4 and kept resurrecting.

**Root files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/worker_foreman.py (~line 131 daemon-dead branch); lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py save_tasks/_save.

**Fix:** Replace the bulk save_tasks call in worker_foreman's daemon-dead branch with individual row-level UPDATEs for only the in_progress tickets being reset (status=sprint, claimed_at=null). This prevents stale reads from overwriting concurrent terminal-status changes (setstatus, close, done).

**Test plan:** Test that (1) ticket is in_progress at load time, (2) setstatus cancels it concurrently before worker_foreman saves, (3) worker_foreman reset logic runs, (4) ticket stays cancelled/closed, not sprint. All existing tests pass.
